problem_statement: The flight details page shows an inflated available seat count because the calculation uses total + booked instead of total - booked.
goal: Fix the seat availability computation so available seats reflect remaining capacity: total seats minus booked seats.
acceptance_criteria: Flight details display available seats as total_seats - booked_seats.
Available seats never exceed aircraft capacity for a flight.
The calculation is corrected at the backend source used by flight details.
constraints: Keep the change localized to the seat inventory availability computation unless tests reveal a broader shared helper is used.
Preserve existing data model and API shape unless absolutely necessary.
unknowns: Whether the incorrect calculation is only in the seat inventory aggregate/query or also mirrored in a service/schema layer.
Whether any reconciliation or seed scripts rely on the same formula and should be updated for consistency.
repo_summary: Backend-heavy repository with flight and seat inventory logic under backend/app/db, backend/app/services, backend/app/models, and backend/app/schemas; issue points specifically to backend/app/db/seat_inventory.py and function seat_inventory_counts.

targeted_symbols.reason: Explicitly identified in the issue as the affected implementation; likely contains the incorrect availability arithmetic.
related_symbols: any availability calculation used by flight details

localization_rationale: The issue description directly names the affected file and function: backend/app/db/seat_inventory.py (seat_inventory_counts).
The bug is a simple arithmetic inversion in available seat computation, so the smallest relevant fix is likely in the backend seat inventory counting logic.
Flight details likely read from this backend helper through a service or route, but those layers are secondary unless the helper is only a thin wrapper.
